findings.justification: The wp_ajax_um_dismiss_notice handler calls only check_ajax_nonce() for CSRF protection but has no current_user_can() capability check, allowing any authenticated user (subscriber, contributor) to dismiss admin security notices stored in the um_hidden_admin_notices option.

The admin_init handler force_dismiss_notice verifies a nonce but performs no capability check, so any logged-in user who can obtain the um-admin-nonce can suppress admin notices by appending um_dismiss_notice and um_admin_nonce to any admin URL.

Notice message content stored in the um_admin_notices option is echoed raw (no escaping) in display_notice(); while individual add_notice() callers use wp_kses(), messages injected via the unfiltered um_adm_action_custom_update_notice filter or future callers can reach the screen without sanitization.

Content returned by the um_adm_action_custom_update_notice filter is placed directly into the notice message without escaping before it is echoed in display_notice(), creating an XSS risk for third-party code that provides HTML in the filter callback.
